name: Mission
description: The Mission is a beaded, slightly overstable midrange that can hold any line. Every company is trying to create the best go-to midrange that holds straight and fades consistently at the end, and this disc certainly holds up to that ideal. Compared with the Flying Squirrel, the Mission feels similar except for the bead.

name: Acacia
description: The Acacia from Above Ground Level seems to be a new putter from Above Ground Level. They are making some unique discs, especially putters, with amazing plastic blends. Look forward to this one. It was approved on July 24th, 2023.

name: Baobab
description: The AGL Discs Baobab is an extremely overstable putt and approach disc golf disc. The Baobab sports a unique flight. This disc does not like to fly and when it does, it likes to fade hard. It has quickly become a favorite among those who have thrown it. The Boabab features a top bead and flat top making it comfortable to throw both backhand and forehand further increasing its versatility. This disc becomes a freight train of a forehand roller disc, hence earning the title the "Bao-Bowler"! Whether you need a long putt to fall quickly to avoid trouble, need a tight s-curve flight, or just need a disc to dump hard the Baobab will take your approach game Above Ground Level.

name: Beech
description: The AGL Discs Beech is an overstable midrange disc with a double bead on it. It's also got a very unique angle from the flight plate down to the edge of the disc, giving you amazing grip when throwing it both forehand and backhand. With the flight numbers of 5/2/0/3 the Beech can definitely handle the power on those flex forehand shots, while still being a reliable overstable backhand mid. Some have called the Beech "the distance Bab". Although it doesn't have as much glide as our other mid-range, it can if one is found with a little dome. Most of the Woodland models came out pretty flat, while the Alpine can have a little dome to them. We are very excited to bring to market a new mold that can be a great compliment to our current lineup. Enjoy that Beech life at AGL Discs!

name: Cedar
description: The AGL Discs Cedar is an overstable distance driver which came onto the scene in March of 2020. The Cedar was molded to replace our very first disc, the now out of production Cypress distance driver. The Cedar is perfect for forehands or for really cranking on your shot with a big anhyzer because it can handle big arm speed, or headwinds. With flight numbers which rate at 11/5/0/3 this disc is long, but predictable. The Cedar is the flagship driver in the AGL lineup, but it is one that can be relied upon. Those who remember the Cypress will find that the Cedar fits nicely into the hole left in the bag when the Cypress went out of production. Take your distance game Above Ground Level and bag your Cedar today.

name: Elm
description: The AGL Elm is a reliable fairway driver with a strong, overstable finish. With flight numbers of 9/4/0/3, it offers a controlled flight that holds its line before finishing with a solid dump as it slows down. It's a dependable option for both backhand and forehand throws and handles wind with ease. The Elm gives players a fairway they can trust to push forward before finishing hard and on-target.

name: Juniper
description: An understable midrange with exceptional glide and easy turn. Ideal for anhyzer lines, hyzer-flips, and players with moderate arm speed looking for distance.

name: Koa
description: The AGL Koa is a dependable putter designed for both throwing and putting. It offers a clean, straight flight with just enough stability to handle power without drifting off line. The shape is comfortable in the hand with a feel that works well for both spin and push styles. It holds the line you put it on and finishes with control, making it a solid choice for putts, approaches, and short drives.

name: Locust
description: The AGL Discs Locust, the newest fairway to our lineup, is a 9-Speed but is also an undercover distance driver. The Locust is quickly becoming one of AGL's best selling discs of all time. We are truly amazed at how FAR this disc flies, and it has been called the farthest flying disc in our lineup. This disc sports flight numbers of 9/4/-1/3 which makes this disc a very versatile fairway driver. The 4 Glide and -1 Turn mean this disc can stand up to some headwinds and still fly true. The Locust also provides that consistent and predictable left to right finish that can make all the difference when lining up a long drive. Whether you are looking for that perfectly placed drive, or just need more confidence in your throw; the Locust will be sure to take your distance game Above Ground Level.

name: Madrone
description: The AGL Discs Madrone is a straight flying, neutral to slightly stable, putt and approach disc golf disc with a micro-bead and a shallow thumb track. These features make this disc comfortable in the hand and give it a smooth flight with a subtle right to left finish for right handed throwers. Sporting flight numbers of 3/3/0/1 this putter has become a staple in people's bags. This disc is torque resistant and can really handle full power off the tee for those shots where you NEED a putter, off the tee. Whether shooting approach shots or sinking those important putts the Madrone will give you the confidence you need to perform Above Ground Level.

name: Magnolia
description: A neutral-to-overstable midrange with consistent, reliable flight. Holds the line on straight shots and finishes with a predictable fade.

name: Manzanita
description: The AGL Discs Manzanita is a stable to slightly over-stable putt and approach disc with a beadless rim, featuring a shallow thumb track. The Manzanita is a go to disc for some headwind putts, for hitting those tight forehand approach shots, and for those backhand shots which require a predictable fade. Featuring flight numbers of 3/3/0/2 the Manzanita is very torque resistant, and we would say "the most stable of the AGL putters". Need a long C2 putt? Maybe a long hyzer shot into a protected pin? Do you have a tricky long headwind putt? Just reach for the Manzanita and take your game Above Ground Level.

name: Maple
description: The AGL Discs Woodland Maple is an overstable approach disc built for control and consistency. With flight numbers of 4/2/0/2, it resists torque and maintains stability on both backhand and forehand throws. The Maple delivers a dependable fade and feels solid in the hand, making it a trusted option for upshots and short drives at any arm speed.

name: Ponderosa
description: The AGL Discs Ponderosa is an understable, GLIDEY putter. The Ponderosa is perfect for beginners and veterans alike, as it really does take a lot of the work out of putting. The flight numbers which ring in at 3/4/-2/0 mean that this disc pops up and flies straight and long for slower arms, and it provides long arcing turn over shots for those with a bit more power. The glide on this disc is CRAZY and means that the ponderosa is also a great circle 2 putter. The Ponderosa is the least stable of the AGL putters with the others in the lineup each adding an additional level of fade. Whether you are new to the game or looking to add a new dimension to your short game, the Ponderosa will take your putts Above Ground Level.

name: Sequoia
description: The AGL Sequoia is a long-distance driver built for controlled power and reliable fade. It holds up to strong throws without turning too much and finishes with a consistent, confident fade. The flight is steady and predictable, which makes it easy to reach for in a wide range of conditions.

name: Spruce
description: A slightly understable midrange that delivers glide and a gentle turn. Versatile enough for wooded fairways and touch shots requiring accuracy.

name: Sycamore
description: A stable fairway driver with a smooth, controlled flight. Reliable in wind and ideal for straight drives with a soft fade at the finish.

name: Orsus
description: The Orsus, Aerobee's inaugural mold, is crafted in France from entirely biosourced organic materials. This midrange disc boasts a domey top and delivers a straight, glide-enhanced flight. Designed as a versatile, one-size-fits-all midrange, the Orsus reliably maintains any line it is thrown on.

name: Apollo
description: The Apollo is the first disc from Alfa Discs. This is a straight flying, beaded midrange that is designed to be the workhorse midrange of your bag. It has very good glide for sneaky distance and is capable of holding any line. The Apollo is a great disc for any player at any level.

name: Atlantis
description: The Atlantis from Alfa Discs is your go-to understable driver for adding substantial distance effortlessly. Its remarkable turn and abundant glide are perfect for players looking to up their distance game. Beginners and Intermediate players will find the Atlantis to be perfect for bombing hyzer flips or long sweeping turnover shots.

name: Cosmic
description: The Cosmic is a dependable fairway driver with high glide and providing a nice consistent flight. This mold will do well as your go-to disc. It is capable of holding a variety of lines from hyzer to anhyzer, making it a highly capable mold for shot shaping, or to get controllable distance.

name: Theios
description: The Theios is the first putter from Alfa Discs. This is a glidey, low speed, easy-to throw putter. It easily flies straight and can handle a large variety of lines. This all-purpose putter works great for putting or for your approach shots.

name: Dragonfly
description: The Dragonfly is an understable midrange disc from Aquaflight. You can trust it with finesse shots down narrow fairways and for turn over shots down the fairway. The Dragonfly also floats, so throw near water with confidence!

name: Osprey Aqua
description: The Osprey is an overstable distance driver. Pull it out for those headwind shots, or when you need big fade at the end of the throw. This PDGA approved disc also floats in water, so have no fear throwing for big distance over the pond!

name: Peace Frog
description: The Peace Frog is a controllable stable putter from Aquaflight's lineup. The Peace Frog has a rounded rim with a micro bead. With a bit of fade at the end, you can putt or approach comfortably with this disc. When thrown with a little power the disc will fly straight at the chains. This putter also floats in water, so have no fear of waters-edge putts!

name: Pelican
description: The Pelican is an understable control driver, perfect for turnover shots, or if thrown with less power, straight shots up the fairway! This workable disc should be a staple for newer players or those with slower arm speed. Plus, it floats in water, so you can have confidence near water hazards.

name: Swift
description: The Swift is the perfect, controllable driver for those with lower arm speed! It will help you achieve max distance and that S-Flight path that you've always wanted. This PDGA approved driver also floats in water, so you can have confidence around water hazards on your course!

name: Bernoulli
description: The Bernoulli Putter is a staple for disc golf enthusiasts, available in both Recycled and Bio-Based plastic options. This putter combines sustainability with top-notch performance, offering a consistent flight and excellent grip for precision putting. Choose from eco-friendly recycled materials or innovative wood-derived plastic to align your play with environmental conservation. Ideal for players who value the planet as much as their game.

name: Einstein
description: The Bernouli Einstein Midrange Disc is your ultimate tool for precision and control on the disc golf course. With flight ratings of Speed 5, Glide 4, Turn -1, and Fade 1, this disc ensures straight, reliable throws with a smooth, predictable flight path. Its slight turnover and gentle fade make it perfect for players of all skill levels, from beginners mastering their midrange game to seasoned pros seeking consistent accuracy. Crafted from sustanable eco friendly plastics, the Einstein offers excellent grip and a comfortable feel, enhancing your confidence with every throw. Elevate your game with the Bernouli Einstein Midrange Disc – where precision meets innovation.

name: Johnson
description: The Bernoulli Johnson is an understable, high glide distance driver from Bernoulli disc golf. Newer players will find great distance with this disc, while more experienced players will find it perfect for long bomb turnover or anhyzer shots. The Johnson is available in eco-friendly plastics!

name: Marvel
description: The Marvel, by Birdie Disc Golf Supply, is a straight to slightly overstable beadless putter and is very consistent for both putting and throwing. The Marvel takes well to spin putts as well as push putts.

name: Reach
description: The Birdie Disc Golf Reach is akin to the classic "Destroyer" in terms of flight numbers, earning its reputation as the "People's Destroyer." While any fast distance driver with flight characteristics similar to the all-time best-selling disc will inevitably draw comparisons, that's precisely where the similarities end. Disc golfers, whether they're just starting out or have years of experience, will appreciate the Reach for its unique ability to straighten and glide over great distances. It's not overly stable, yet it reliably returns, providing the expected finish for this type of driver every time.

name: Rise
description: The Birdie Rise is a straight to overstable midrange disc designed for reliable performance in all conditions. Ideal for precise, controlled shots. The Rise has a little more push compared to the Ultra.

name: Strike
description: The Strike by Birdie Disc Golf Supply is an excellent choice for flip-up forehands and power backhands. It is an ideal choice for all skill levels when it comes to control. Lower arm speed players will find this disc useful inside of 300 feet for shots with a reliable finish while bigger arms will reach for The Strike inside of 400 feet for consistent laser beam shots.

name: Ultra
description: Experience the power and reliability of the Birdie Disc Golf Ultra, a midrange disc that delivers outstanding performance on the course. Designed to push without flipping and with consistent fade, the Ultra will become a go-to choice for many players. Endorsed by YouTube sensations Anthony Bodanza and Broderic Thompson, this disc has garnered praise from top players like Alex Geisinger, known for his impressive throwing skills. Geisinger raves about the Ultra's torque resistance and precise flight path, making it the perfect addition to his bag.

name: Weapon
description: The Birdie Disc Golf Weapon is a unique 8-speed disc offering a perfect balance of glide and overstability. Designed to bridge the gap between overstable utility 9-speeds and glidey 7-speeds, the Weapon delivers versatile performance that disc golfers can rely on. With its incredible combination of distance and control, this disc is built to handle a variety of shots and conditions, making it a standout addition to any bag.

name: Berzerker
description: The Black Zombie Berzerker is a high-speed driver designed for pros seeking a long throw with a reliable comeback, even in strong headwinds. With its fast and predictable flight, it also serves as an excellent choice for amateur players looking for a consistent driver. Known for its ability to boom down the fairway while maintaining stability, the Berzerker has been likened to the Xcaliber, offering extended distance with a reliable fade.

name: Chainsaw
description: The Black Zombie Chainsaw is a point and shooter putter boasting plenty of glide. This disc will work for everyone- from seasoned pros to fresh zombie hoard recruits. Many people also find the Chainsaw useful for mid-range drives that go straight and land soft.

name: Shotgun
description: The Black Zombie Disc Golf Shotgun has been a trusted favorite among disc golfers for over 5 years, and now it's back in a premium edition. Featuring exceptional grip and a point-and-shoot design, the Shotgun is perfect for players looking to unleash accurate and powerful shots. With flight ratings of 5-5-0-2, this disc is a must-have for your disc golf bag, helping you hunt down those birdies with precision.

name: War Hammer
description: The Black Zombie War Hammer is a beadless putter that compliments the Chainsaw. It's a point and shoot putter with slight overstability, making it a very reliable putter for any type of player.

name: Juju
description: The Juju is an ultra-straight putter. It has innovative thumb tracks around the rim that allow you to adjust your grip in order to adjust the flight of the disc. Like the Precision midrange, the thumb tracks are located at different levels along the rim, and an empty slot is left for when the thumb track is not needed. The Juju is a very beginner friendly disc for those who are new to the game and who are learning how to properly grip putters.

name: Precision
description: If you're looking for a stable, straight flying mid range disc with Excellerator thumb grips and minimal fade, the Precision is the disc for you. The precision is a great disc for making precisions throws and accurate approach shots. This is a tall disc golf disc (2.2cm in height) with a very deep rim (1.3cm). The Precision is a great first midrange disc for beginners. Thumb grips are located at different levels, or there is an open space with no grips at all. Find the groove that makes your throws comfortable and controlled.

name: Sombra
description: Chumba Discs proudly unveils their inaugural mold, the Sombra! This exquisite beadless putter boasts a straight flight path and captivating UV print artwork, with its name "Sombra" translating to "shadow" in Spanish.

name: Belleair
description: The Climo Disc Golf Belleair is a beaded midrange with a straight flight path and effortless glide. Designed for control and consistency, this disc excels in tight fairways and precision approach shots. With a smooth release and reliable stability, the Belleair provides a balanced feel for players who need accuracy and confidence in their midrange game.

name: Champ
description: The Climo Disc Golf Champ is a neutral-flying putter with a microbead, designed for precision on the green and control off the tee. With plenty of glide and a smooth release, this putter delivers a point-and-shoot flight for confident putting. Its torque resistance makes it just as effective for approach shots and short drives, providing consistency in all situations.

name: Cliff
description: The Climo Disc Golf Cliff is a beadless putter rooted in disc golf history. Named after Cliff Stephens Park, the lifelong home course of Ken Climo, it reflects the place where his game was shaped during the 1980s and 1990s and where he still plays today. The Cliff features a straight, glide-forward flight with a slightly lower profile than the Champ, helping produce clean releases and dependable control on putts and approaches.

name: Osprey
description: The Climo Disc Golf Osprey is a versatile 10-speed driver that delivers easy distance and smooth, controllable flight. With a touch of understability, the Osprey excels at long turnover shots, hyzer-flips, and technical line shaping. Ideal for a wide range of players, it offers accessible glide for newer throwers and precision control for advanced players looking to finesse their way through the fairway.

name: Skyway
description: The Climo Disc Golf Skyway is an 11-speed distance driver with impressive glide and a touch of overstable control. Designed for both power throwers and developing players, this disc delivers reliable distance with a consistent finish. Advanced players will appreciate its ability to handle headwinds and high-speed throws, while newer players will find it dependable for controlled hyzer shots.

name: Streak
description: The Climo Streak is a fairway driver built for consistency, control, and clean lines. With a stable flight and dependable fade, it's a disc you can trust to hold the line and finish predictably. As the first fairway driver in the Climo Disc Golf lineup, the Streak is designed to be a go-to option for shaping repeatable shots off the tee or in the fairway. Inspired by Ken Climo's legendary nine-title run, the Streak is all about doing it right- and doing it again.

name: Company
description: The Company is a high-speed, overstable distance driver from Crosslap for advanced players who want to crank it with maximum force for maximum distance.

name: Credo
description: You can count on German Engineering to produce quality products, and when it comes to golf discs, the Crosslap Credo is no exception. This overstable putter has a unique shape and feel with an ultra flat top. The Credo is a tall putt and approach disc with a wide diameter. This disc is available in two incredible plastic blends. Basic plastic consists of a firm but grippy mid-grade blend while Advanced Soft has an almost elastic rubber feel.

name: Lucky
description: The Lucky is an understable midrange available in unique Crosslap plastic blends. This is a great disc for beginners, especially in light weights.

name: Openwater
description: The Openwater distance driver is slightly understable upon release, pulling away from the fade until the end of its flight when it finishes with a moderate fade. It will be a very dependable, straight driver in the 250-350 foot range and will be a great addition to your disc golf bag. If you've got the power, then hyzer-flip the Openwater for even more distance!

name: Shadowplay
description: The Shadowplay is a low profile, understable putter with a smooth, comfortable outer rim design, that also offers a textured inner rim. This design makes the putter unique and stand out from others on the market.

name: Vigil
description: The Vigil is a speed-8 driver that is designed for beginners. It can be handled by new players, both young and old.

name: Albatross
description: Score an albatross with the Albatross. That's right. We said it. Check out this super-fast large winged driver. The Albatross has a high speed rating of 14, and will soar great distances, gliding over all obstacles to reach its nesting grounds on par-4 greens. Feel free to throw it frequently and throw it hard!

name: Beaver
description: The Beaver is a straight fliying putter with a small bead available in Daredevil's durable Flex Performance plastic.

name: Bigfoot
description: The Daredevil Bigfoot is an ultra fast driver. This disc has the largest rim width allowed by the PDGA at 2.5 cm. I don't know if you need big feet to throw this disc, but you certainly need a big arm. This disc will go far for power players and will be a great utility disc for slower arm speeds.

name: Bighorn
description: The Beaver, Bighorn, Great Horned Owl, Polar Bear, and Woodchuck are all the same mold. The names in DareDevil's case just signify the plastic. This is the Grip Performance version of their beaded putter, with a stiffer blend than the Polar Bear.

name: Caribou
description: The Caribou is a slightly understable midrange available in Grip Performance plastic.

name: Gray Jay
description: Check out this super-fast large-wing driver. The Gray Jay has a high speed rating of 14, and will soar great distances, gliding over all obstacles to reach its nesting grounds with a hard, overstable fade. Feel free to throw it frequently and throw it hard!

name: Great Horned Owl
description: It would be a wise consideration to start throwing the Great Horned Owl around the green. Dressed in High Performance Plastic and using Daredevil Discs' popular and dependable putter mold, the Great Horned Owl comes to the course as a more rigid and fast, stable putter. This disc will brush off the wind as it flies true and confident.

name: Grizzly
description: The Grizzly is an overstable midrange that provides precision accuracy. It's got a little microbead that provides a clean release. This disc is available in Daredevil's high performance plastic.

name: Hellbender
description: The Daredevil Hellbender is an overstable putt and approach disc. This disc has similar flight numbers to the Grizzly, but has a feel more akin to a Zone. The Hellbender flies straight out of the hand and has a reliable fade at the end of the flight.

name: Moose
description: The Moose and Grizzly are close cousins, sharing the microbeaded design. The Moose will be a little more overstable, and have considerable glide.

name: Ogopogo
description: Produced in soft plastic usually reserved for putters, the Ogopogo takes all the great qualities of the Sabertooth, Mammoth and Buffalo, and gives the thrower one more; stopping ability. The Ogopogo doesn't skip on grass, cement or dirt and when it hits a tree, it just drops and stops. Because of the nature of the Elastic Performance Plastic, this disc will beat in quickly to become understable.

name: Polar Bear
description: The Polar Bear shares the same mold as the Beaver, Bighorn, Great Horned Owl, Polar Bear, and Woodchuck. The names in DareDevil's case just signify the plastic. This is the Grip Performance version of their beaded putter.

name: Sabertooth
description: The Sabertooth is an overstable fairway driver for those shots in the wind, or when you need to get around those sharp corners. Available in Daredevil's High Performance plastic, the Sabertooth is durable and will get you where you need to go.

name: Swift Fox
description: Not only does the Swift Fox have a cool name, but this is a great Daredevil disc for newer disc golfers. The understable nature of this fairway driver makes it easy to throw.

name: Walrus
description: The Walrus is a unique mid-range in the Daredevil Discs' Elastic Performance Plastic. The Walrus has a shot shaping stability that does not require a lot of arm speed to unlock its magic. It has surprising glide and a dependable fade that will perform well on many holes, courses and lines.

name: Wolverine
description: The Wolverine is a straight flying, dependable fairway driver. It is available in high performance plastic.

name: Aftershock
description: The Aftershock is a solid overstable disc in the DGA lineup. While not quite as overstable as the Shockwave, the Aftershock still offers a consistent and predictable fading flight path while also providing more distance. This midrange can handle powerful throws and will keep its line for almost the entire flight path. The disc features a microbead, increasing the stability slightly and offering more durability.

name: Avalanche
description: The DGA Avalanche is a flat-topped overstable disc that offers reliable fade and resists high-speed turn, making it a great choice for any player looking for an accurate and predictable flight. Its low-profile rim ensures a comfortable grip and effortless release for both backhand and forehand throwers, making it an excellent addition to any disc golf bag.

name: Banzai
description: The Banzai is said to be the overstable counterpart to the popular Pipeline. (Both disc names are derived from the iconic Hawaiian surf break, the Banzai Pipeline.) The Banzai is a fairway driver with speed, glide, and the stability you need in a trustworthy disc. This disc has a very straight flight, with a predictable fade at the end. Perfect for backhand or forehand drives, this disc will do it all!

name: Blast
description: The DGA Blast has a shallow profile, subtle thumb track, and slightly recessed flight plate for a comfortable feel and a clean release on both backhand and forehand throws.

name: Blowfly I
description: The Blowfly 1 takes "super soft" discs to a new level. This disc is not only soft, but flexible and bendable. The Blowfly 1 has a slightly overstable flight to it. This disc travels at slow speeds and stops where it lands without sliding much. If you like soft putters, this is a disc you'll want in your bag. The Blowfly 1 has an interesting shape that feels very comfortable for a fangrip, but strange for a powergrip. (Who knows why you'd want to power grip a disc like this anyways). The Blowfly 1 and the Blunt Gumbputt have a very similar feel.

name: Blunt Gumbputt
description: For a putter with an almost rubber-band soft feel, the Blunt Gumbputt is fantastic! This disc falls straight down after it hits any target, making it one of the best short-range (and long range) putters that you could ask for. Of the super-soft putters on the market, the Gumbputt was the very first and it was Steady Ed's (the founder of disc golf) putter of choice. The Blowfly 1 is the same disc but with bubbles in the plastic, similar to Blizzard Champion.

name: Breaker
description: The Breaker is a putt and approach disc in the DGA line. For a putter, this disc has a relatively low profile and a smooth flat top. This is an overstable beaded putter and the low profile gives it a natural fit in your hand, especially for forehands. This is an excellent putt and approach disc for windy conditions, short drives, and forehand approach shots. The Breaker won't flip over with powerful throws.

name: Hellfire
description: The DGA Hellfire is a massively overstable meathook fairway driver with very little glide. This is a utility disc designed for overhand shots, powerful flex lines, and getting out of trouble. This disc also excels in even the strongest headwind.

name: Hurricane
description: The DGA Hurricane is a fairly stable high speed distance driver with a wide rim and excellent glide. For power throwers, this disc will go further than any disc DGA makes. For beginner and intermediate players, the Hurricane will fly overstable. The Hurricane is a good disc for both forehand and backhand throws.

name: Hypercane
description: The Hypercane is a theoretical apocalyptic level hurricane the size of the US that may have killed some dinosaurs. It's very fitting that this disc is a super-fast, overstable distance driver for the most powerful of arms. The rim width is similar to the Hurricane, but the fade is even greater. The Hypercane will bring overstability to any backhand or forehand throw. Professionals will find amazing distance and predictability, while amateur players will find a perfect, overstable driver for placing their utility shots with ease.

name: Pipeline
description: The DGA Pipeline is a straight flying, controllable fairway driver. It is used for gliding through tight fairways, backhand or forehand.

name: Quake
description: Add some reliability to your mid-range game with the DGA Quake. It is overstable and also trustworthy in all wind conditions. This mid-range will hold a consistent flight path when thrown both backhand and forehand. The Quake features a super comfortable rim without sharp edges and also features a very flat top, ensuring an easy release for all hand sizes. This is one of the very best midranges for forehands because of its flat top, comfortable rim, and perfect overstability.

name: Rift
description: If you are looking for a point-and-shoot midrange with a clean, smooth feel in your hand, then look no further than the DGA Rift. This is an all-purpose midrange disc that will earn a spot in the bag for players at any level.

name: Rogue
description: The Rogue is an incredibly straight flying disc for how fast it is. It's one of the fastest discs on the market with the amazing turn and fade flight ratings of -1 1.

name: Sail
description: The Sail is an understable distance driver from DGA, meant to provide a wide range of players a disc with a wide array of possible uses. With a high glide rating, this driver will allow new players to increase their driving distance, as the Sail will remain aloft with lower speed drives. This disc is great for shots meant to end without fading back.

name: Sonar
description: The DGA Sonar is a beaded, overstable putter with a large shoulder. The Sonar will always have a reliable finish at the end of it's flight, or a nice hook at the end of long putts.

name: Squall
description: For a solid throw with a bit of personality to its flight, the Squall by DGA fits the bill. This disc has a smooth flight with a hint of turn and slightly stronger fade before it sits down. The disc has an average to thin sized rim, and has a great feel for making a nice midrange or approach shot.

name: Steady
description: The all new DGA Steady is a superior putt and approach disc that pays homage to "Steady" Ed Headrick, the founder of Disc Golf. Headrick was known as "Steady" because of his steady hand and consistent putting ability. This is a beaded putter that has a hyzer finish at slow speeds. It also has a relatively straight flight path when thrown with some power.

name: Steady BL
description: The DGA Steady BL is a beadless putter with a similar flight path to the original Steady. With higher speeds the Steady BL exhibits a straight flight path and at lower speeds it will finish on a hyzer. This putter allows for a smooth release due to its clean and beadless finish.

name: Surf
description: The DGA Surf is a putter with a neutral flight and a shallow feeling in the hand. It is beadless and has a slimmer profile than other DGA putters. It's a pefect putter for those looking for more glide.

name: Tempest
description: The Tempest is an understable long distance driver. Featuring excellent glide and control, you will achieve pinpoint accuracy on the longest of throws. Newer players will find extra distance and advanced players will find predictable turn and glide with an understable finish.

name: Torrent
description: DGA entered the warp speed driver market with the introduction of the Torrent. This distance driver is really fast, with a 14 speed and a really thick rim. DGA says that this is easily the fastest disc in their lineup and will "amaze with its speed out of your hand. Advanced throwers will love the added distance this disc will give their game, while pro caliber players will trust its increased glide and fade at the finish."

name: Tremor
description: The Tremor is an understable midrange that has pretty good glide and creates smooth straight-to-anhyzer lines. Advanced players will use this as a touchy turnover or a replacement for lacking in the forehand department. For beginning players this will finally be a disc that flies straight.

name: Tsunami
description: The DGA Tsunami is a high speed overstable disc golf driver. This is a good disc for power throwers looking for distance and consistency, and makes a good disc for sidearm/forehand throws for golfers who don't want an ultra thick rim.

name: Typhoon
description: The DGA Typhoon is a high-speed distance driver built for effortless distance and controlled turnovers. Designed to blend speed, glide, and finesse, the Typhoon allows players to shape long turnover shots and execute precise flex lines with confidence. Whether you're attacking the fairway with power or dialing in technical placement shots, this disc delivers a smooth, consistent flight with excellent control.

name: Vortex
description: The Vortex is the first disc in the Catrina Allen lineup! The Vortex is a speed 7 disc that is easy to control and will hold any line with ease. It has excellent glide and just the right amount of turn for newer players and veterans alike.

name: Allosaurus
description: The new Allosaurus was added to the Dino Discs lineup in the summer of 2023. As a "speed 12" disc, it will require more armspeed to get maximum distance results. For youngsters, it will test their power, or it can be a disc that they grow into. For other players who typically struggle with distance drivers, the Allosaurus is a great lightweight choice with a lot of glide that can build confidence. NOTE: The Allosaurus used to be made by Gateway and was the 'Journey" mold. The 2023 Allosaurus is a new mold made by Dino Discs themselves and uses the Egg Shell plastic.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: The Brachiosaurus is an understable midrange disc, which makes it the ideal beginner disc. As a very lightweight disc, it is even more perfect for the complete novice or child disc golfer. The rim also has a low profile to comfortably fit smaller hands. This is an easy choice for your youngster to give disc golf a try. NOTE: The Brachiosaurus is molded from the ABC Flying Squirrel.

name: Diplodocus
description: The Diplodocus is an easy-to-throw fairway driver disc that is ideal for young players or new disc golfers who want a disc that is easy to handle. The rim is comfortable for small hands and it is also an understable disc, meaning that it more fade-resistant for straight flight paths. The Diplodocus may or may not have a dimpled center on the top of the disc.

name: Pterodactyl
description: The Pterodactyl is a manageable driver for young disc golfers. The rim isn't too wide, thus a high-speed release is not necessary. However, it still lands in the "distance driver" category and provides a great gateway to longer, more impressive throws down the fairway. Like other Dino Discs, it is lightweight, floats in water, and is aimed at young players or those players without a lot of arm speed. NOTE: The Pterodactyl is made with the mold for the ABC Secret Weapon.

name: Spinosaurus
description: The Spinosaurus is a "12 speed" driver, meaning it needs a little more armspeed than other Dino Discs to use its full potential. This disc is a great step up for a kid or a beginner who is "turning over" their other Dino Discs (when they throw hard the disc ends up rolling or veering off way to the right for right handed players). This is also a great disc for kids to grow into or use as a first forehand disc. NOTE: The Spinosaurus used to be a 9 speed fairway driver that used Gateway's Spear Mold. The Spinosaurus is now a unique mold made by Dino Discs themselves and is a 12 speed driver.

name: Stegosaurus
description: The Stegosaurus is the quintessential mid-range driver designed for some turn with a little bit of end-fade. Of course, in a lighter weight, it is more understable than the flight numbers might suggest. But for kids, this mid will do the trick in fading to the basket for a short putt. It has a somewhat deeper rim for more of a "frisbee" feel for new players. NOTE: The Stegosaurus is molded from the ABC Mission.

name: Triceratops
description: The Triceratops is a great lightweight beginner putter that kids will love chucking at the chains. It can also be used as a stand-alone disc for any youngster who is learning how to throw. If you want to buy a single disc for a kid to learn with, this is the one. NOTE: This disc is made from the ABC Money putter mold.

name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
description: The Tyrannosaurus Rex was the king of the carnivorous dinosaurs, so it is only fitting that the disc should be the fastest in the Dino Discs line-up. This is a maximum distance driver with a wide rim. So, even though it is very lightweight, it might be more difficult for very small children to get it up to speed. But once a player gets their technique figured out and they want to try to let loose a long-bomb drive at maximum speed, this is the disc to unleash! NOTE: The Tyrannosaurus Rex is made with the mold for the ABC Bee Line.

name: CD1
description: The Discmania CD1 is a versatile control driver. The CD1 puts you in the driver's seat, allowing you full control to manipulate and style the disc exactly how you want it to. This disc is a great choice for both less powerful or less skilled players looking for a first distance driver and experienced experts seeking a reliable, versatile disc for their game. The CD1's execution feels effortless and controlled, often competing for distance with even the fastest drivers in experienced hands. The CD1 can handle just about any wind condition with ease, but is best suited for tailwinds and calmer weather. Players will appreciate the CD1's ability to fly similarly to a well-seasoned PD, offering the right amount of glide and shot shaping options while maintaining enough stability to end with a smooth low-speed fade. If you only had one disc to play a round with, the CD1 would be an excellent choice for its versatility and reliability.

name: DD
description: The new Discmania DD is the most understable distance driver in the Originals lineup. The new version made in Sweden is significantly more understable and beginner friendly than the older Innova-made ones. This disc will do wonders in the hands of beginners and intermediate players and will be a easy disc for them to throw far, even with low power. Advanced players will find this disc to be understable enough to turn over and finish right even with a slight hyzer release. This makes this disc great for rollers, sky anhyzers, and slow flip-ups with some touch.

name: DD1
description: Considered to be the younger sibling of the DD3, the DD1 distance driver by Discmania offers a less overstable flight path than the popular DD3 distance driver. The DD1's smooth glide carries it to the horizon with some turn, before reliably fading back at the end of its flight. This versatile disc is a top choice for both accurate drives in the woods and full-power throws in the open fields. The DD1 distance driver is truly a disc for the masses, designed by Discmania to deliver consistent performance and control for disc golfers of all skill levels.

name: DD2
description: The Discmania DD2 —formerly known as the Enigma—is now part of the Originals line, offering speed, glide, and effortless distance. Designed for maximum flight potential, the DD2 delivers a fast, smooth release with a controlled fade , making it a go-to option for big-distance throwers.

name: DD3
description: The Discmania DD3 is the new disc for distance. Eagle McMahon crushes this disc down the fairway. The "Cloudbreaker" is the title name for the Swirly Star Signature edition. This is a reliable, max-distance driver for the more advanced arms that may still offer some extra distance to intermediate players as well.

name: Drop
description: The Discmania Drop is an overstable approach disc designed to handle high power, snap, and torque resistance. Its low profile and reduced glide ensure a predictable, consistent fade on every shot. Built to be more overstable than the Tactic, the Drop excels in controlled distance and reliable finishes, making it an ideal choice for approaches in any condition. Whether throwing hard for a strong finish or softly for precision, the Drop provides confidence and consistency.

name: Enigma
description: The Discmania Evolution Enigma is the first Distance Driver and fourth disc to be released in Discmania's Evolution Line, manufactured by Latitude 64. The Enigma is a high speed driver that will provide big distance for experienced disc golfers.

name: Essence
description: The Essence, a do-it-all fairway driver in the Evolution lineup, shines with its understable nature. It is especially ideal for players seeking their first distance driver, while seasoned pros will also find it valuable for a variety of shots. When faced with a technical hole on the course, the Essence is the disc you'll reach for!

name: FD
description: The FD is truly one of the great straight-flying fairway drivers. This disc is perfect for straight line shots through low ceiling tree lines. As an accurate, smooth flying disc, the FD is a winner for beginners and pros. While the FD isn't the fastest flying fairway driver, it does offer excellent glide, allowing it to rival your furthest flying distance drivers. The Discmania made and Innova made FDs feel and fly similar, if not identical. When adding a review, feel free to comment your opinions on how they differ!

name: FD1
description: Discmania's FD1 is a new mold made only from Discmania's factory in Sweden. It's the perfect choice for players looking for a straight flight with dependable fade. It fills the gap between the FD and FD3, making it an excellent option for accuracy on the fairways.

name: FD3
description: The Discmania line-up gets a much-anticipated addition in the form of a beefy fairway driver. The newcomer is called the FD3 and it is targeted to everyone who can appreciate an overstable driver that's trustworthy in all conditions. The FD3 features a flat profile and a very comfortable rim. Despite being very close to the Power Driver (PD) in terms of flight ratings, the FD3 offers a noticeably more overstable flight pattern. This disc can be used for everything from touch sidearms that get you out of trouble to full power drives off the tee. You can rely on the FD3's long but powerful and forward-penetrating fade.

name: Founder
description: The Discmania Founder is an understable midrange designed for smooth turnovers, hyzer-flips, and controlled touch shots. With high glide and a gentle flight, it's a great option for players with lower arm speeds or anyone looking to shape technical lines with minimal effort. The Founder offers easy distance and consistent feel, making it a versatile tool for shot shaping and finesse.

name: Function
description: The Discmania Function is an easy-to-throw fairway driver offering the most understable flight path of any Discmania disc produced to date. Players who appreciate the performance of the Essence but desire a disc with even more flip will quickly fall in love with the Function. This driver is engineered for players with lower arm speeds, beginners, amateurs, and anyone who enjoys the flight of an understable fairway driver that executes turning shots quickly and effortlessly.

name: Glacier
description: The Discmania Glacier is a signature midrange designed in collaboration with Gannon Buhr, built for clean lines and consistent control. With input from Gannon himself, the Glacier offers a balanced blend of glide, stability, and comfort in hand, making it a go-to option for technical midrange shots. Whether navigating woods or throwing flat and straight in the open, it delivers dependable results without overthinking the release. A modern workhorse for players who value precision over flash.

name: Instinct
description: Introducing the Discmania Instinct, the inaugural disc in Discmania's Evolution line crafted by Latitude 64. As a stable fairway driver, the Instinct is an ideal choice for executing straight shots along the fairway with a subtle fade towards the end.

name: Link
description: The Discmania Link, the first putter in Discmania's Evolution line by Latitude 64. This neutral-flying disc excels both as a close-range putter inside the circle or for approaches all the way to a powerful drive off the tee. With a rounded rim featuring a large but comfortable bead, the Link offers a clean feel in the hand that accommodates various grips, including putter grip, fan grip, or power grip.

name: Logic
description: The Logic is the beadless compliment to the Link. This disc has a smooth, comfortable rim and a good height. It's not too deep, not too shallow, just right. The Logic excells in the circle, but can also be used for all of the other putter needs you may have.

name: Maestro
description: The Discmania Maestro is a stable midrange designed for precision control shots. It is part of Discmania's Active Line, and was formerly known as the "Spring Ox".

name: Magician
description: The Discmania Magician is a stable flying all-purpose fairway driver. This disc can handle some power and will always finish its flight with a consistent fade. It is part of Discmania's Active Line, and was formerly known as the "Fox Spirit".

name: Majesty
description: The Majesty is a true 13-speed disc, meant for slightly more advanced players. This disc will give an intermediate player a chance to experience the coveted S-turn, and advanced players a slightly understable driver that will hold an anhyzer nicely or perform beautiful hyzer flips. The Majesty will excel in tailwinds, moderate headwind, backhand, forehand, and roller shots. The Premium version of the Majesty will naturally be more overstable than the base version.

name: MD1
description: The MD1 is a new version of the MD that is slightly faster, flatter topped, and can take a little more power. The MD1 will hold as straight as an arrow for as far as you can throw it.

name: MD3
description: The legendary MD3 by Discmania is a workhorse mid-range disc that has become an icon in the Discmania lineup. Loved by beginners and pros alike, the MD3 offers reliability, control, and versatility. With a balanced flight and predictable fade, this disc delivers precise shots and confident finishes. Its durable plastic ensures consistent performance, making it a must-have for disc golfers at any skill level. Note: The Innova made MD3 and Discmania made MD3 feel and fly nearly identical. Some of the flight numbers on the Innova made MD3 are 5 5 0 3 or 5 5 0 2, but even those runs don't fly significantly different than the newer Swedish made ones. Feel free to comment in the reviews on your experiece!

name: MD4
description: Lower your score with the MD4! This disc is the overstable brother of the MD3. The MD4 has a blunt nose with that standard MD3 micro-bead, giving it a familiar feel in the hand. Not only is the MD4 overstable, but it also has a surprising amount of glide, allowing your shots to fade more while still maintaining predictability. Whether you're facing a stiff wind, looking for a controlled hyzer line, or throwing a nice touch sidearm, the MD4 has you covered. We find this disc to be much more versatile than most other overstable mid-ranges on the market due to the mix of stability and glide. Don't wait, grab yourself an MD4 and lower your score!

name: MD5
description: The MD5 is overstable. No wind or shaky release will change that. If you are looking for a dependable, no questions asked, goes where you tell it to go midrange with an awesome feel, look no further. NOTE: The Innova made and Discmania made MD5's are VASTLY different in hand feel. The Innova made ones felt similar to a Gator, with a large, chunky rim and a bead. The Discmania made ones feel much more like a modern midrange. The shape of the rim is comparable to a 5 speed Zone. Please specify which one you are talking about in the reviews. For reference, the specifications for the Innova-made MD5 are D: 21.1; H: 1.9; W: 1.4; D: 1.3.

name: Mentor
description: The Discmania Mentor is a slightly understable, moderately high speed distance driver. This disc promises distance to those with slightly slower arm speeds. It is part of Discmania's Active Line, and was formerly known as the "Sea Serpent".

name: Method
description: The Discmania Method is the second release in the Discmania Evolution line manufactured by Latitude 64, and the first midrange disc. The Method is a workhorse, beaded, overstable midrange disc. Coming in at 5, 5, 0, 3, the Method will hold up to your power and finish with reliable fade. With it's high glide, it'll carry itself down the fairway with any flex shots before coming out in the end. After getting his hands on it, Simon Lizotte simply stated, "This is the best midrange I've tried."

name: Mutant
description: The Discmania Mutant is an extremely overstable midrange. It features low glide and a hard fade, providing consistency in most windy conditions. This is a utility disc that can help you get out of those sketchy situations and back on track. The Mutant was designed from a thought experiment to see if you could make a disc with only straight lines. The result is a unique disc that is known as the "Cybertruck of midranges."

name: Notion
description: The Discmania Notion is the epitome of smooth control and glide, making it one of the straightest flying putter in the Evolution line. Ideal for players who appreciate easy shot manipulation, it excels at carving tee or approach shots through wooded areas and feels comfortable for those who prefer a shallower grip. Perfect for putting on a straighter line with minimal effort, the Notion shines off the tee, shaping turnovers and offering buttery flip-up to turn shots. It thrives in tailwinds or when the straightest path is needed. Whether you need to nail a straight shot or master tricky turnovers, the Notion is your go-to putter for sealing the deal.

name: Origin
description: The Origin is a small diameter midrange with big potential. The nose has a smooth, rounded feel with a flat microbead on the bottom. The Origin is understable when thrown at high speeds, making it the perfect disc for long turnover shots, as well as precise gentle lines through the woods. It thrives in tailwinds, wooded courses, and calm conditions at any course. This is the perfect complementary disc to the Essence.

name: P1
description: For a truly straight-flying putter, it doesn't get much better than the Discmania P1 – Maniac. This disc has turn and fade ratings at 0, which makes it a great straight-line approach disc and chain-rattling putter.

name: P1X
description: The Discmania P1X is an ultra straight flying putt and approach disc with a bead. This putter is designed with a unique round nose to give you that perfect feel in your hand that will help you make more putts. This disc is out of production and it's unclear if Discmania will ever make it again.

name: P2
description: The P2 is a straight flying, but slightly overstable putter made by Discmania. This disc is stable enough that it can handle headwind throws and fairly long approach shots. This putter was designed specifically for pro players, but can be useful for disc golfers of all skill levels. This was the first disc that Discmania brought back when they took over their own production.

name: P2X
description: The Discmania P2x is a straight-to-slightly-overstable putter crafted in collaboration with Gannon Buhr as part of the Originals line. Designed to be a versatile, all-around putter, the P2x excels in both approach shots and putting, offering a dependable, straight flight you can rely on in any situation. After extensive rounds of prototyping and refining, the P2x combines comfort and control, making it the disc you reach for when the stakes are high and precision matters most.

name: P3X
description: The P3x has been re-approved by Discmania as of Nov 9, 2023. Fans can have it back in the bag, and newcomers to the Discmania Originals line can have their beaded putter they've been wanting. The P3x is a flat, overstable, beaded putter. In the hand, it feels like an Aviar3 with a bead. This putt/approach mold is great for shorter wind penetrating shots. Try a P3x if you want your upshot to have a consistent fading finish.

name: P4
description: The Discmania P4 is a dependable putter designed for stability and control. With a resistance to high-speed turn and a strong, predictable fade, this disc excels on forehand approaches, headwind putts, and controlled drives . Its shallow profile ensures a clean grip for all hand sizes, providing comfort and confidence on every throw.

name: Paradigm
description: The paradigm is a higher speed driver that can feel like you are throwing a fairway driver. It feels very comfortabe in the hand, and its understability allows you to carve some great anhyzer shots. That being said, it is still stable enough to be thrown straight, and can still be thrown on a hyzer line without turning the wrong way.

name: PD
description: The Discmania PD (Power Driver) is a great addition to any disc golfer's bag. The wide rim, fast flight, and overstability of the disc allows you to set this disc sailing with a fast spin on a steep anhyzer and watch it pull a beautiful s-curve as it soars through the air. As it beats in, this disc will become a straight flying, dependable go-to.

name: PD2
description: The PD2 is a power driver in the Discmania disc line. It is abnormally fast for the stability it offers. Targeted for the strong-armed touring pro, this disc is very reliable with a backhand, forehand and overhead throws in pretty much any condition.

name: Rockstar
description: The Rockstar is a beginner-friendly fairway driver from Active Line. Designed with the player in mind, this disc allows easy access to begin opening up your game to untapped distance and control. Golfers with more experience and power will find this disc to be moderately understable and will display more high-speed turn from the flight. Beginner to intermediate level players are going to enjoy the smooth nature of it's flight patterns, superb gliding ability, and adaptability to both forehand and backhand throws. The Rockstar features a thin rounded nose with gentle wing on the underside of the disc, making for an extremely comfortable fit in your hand.

name: Sensei
description: The Discmania Sensei is a low speed, neutral flying, all purpose putt and approach disc. It is part of Discmania's Active Line, and was formerly known as the "Tiger Warrior".

name: Shogun
description: Shogun, the first ever beaded putter available in Active Line. Designed to withstand a good amount of power, this deep putter provides a comfortable ergonomic feel and reliable flight when thrown off the tee and for putt/approach. The Shogun will provide relatively straight flight patterns with very minimal low speed fade, allowing it to withstand moderate wind resistance with point-and-shoot accuracy. The Active plastic is the perfect balance between keeping the disc firm, as well as soft to the touch, meaning the disc always feels comfortable and natural in the hand.

name: Splice
description: The Discmania Splice is a fairway driver with a lot of overstability. This disc is great for headwind shots and skip shots. It's used effectively by both backhand and forehand players.

name: Spore
description: Crafted for enjoyment, the Discmania Spore is a versatile disc perfect for various scenarios, whether you're on the disc golf course or just having fun elsewhere. Many players agree that a game of catch is an excellent way to fine-tune your throwing technique before starting a round. The Soft Neo Spore is designed for this very purpose, offering a comfortable catch and the capability to execute the throws you desire. Ideal for executing precise technical upshots, engaging in pre-tournament play, or simply tossing around with friends at the park, the Spore adds a playful twist to your disc golf experience.

name: Tactic
description: The Tactic is designed for tactical approach shots to the basket. It has a flat top and an overstable end fade. In Exo (soft and hard) plastic, it also has a good grip for precision placement. In premium blends like Meta and Vapor, it is more durable and will keep its flight characteristics longer.

name: Tailor
description: The Discmania Tailor is a workhorse midrange that you'll need several of. It's straight as an arrow with a touch of fade for dependability. The disc has a slight dome to it that makes for incredible glide, and it has a smooth, beadless hand feel that makes for confident shots backhand or forehand.

name: TD
description: The classic TD distance driver is making a triumphant return, now redesigned to enhance the playing experience for disc golf enthusiasts of every level. Once known as the "Rush," this beloved model offers an impressively neutral flight path, providing the flexibility needed for mastering a variety of shots. Among its peers in the Originals line, the TD stands out with its exceptionally smooth contours, ensuring a comfortable grip every time. Its design features a gradual shoulder, a rounded nose, and a seamless transition to the lower part of the wing, making it the perfect choice for those who prefer a disc that feels natural and easy to handle, without the harsh edges often found on other models.

name: Vanguard
description: Discmania's Vanguard sets a new standard of reliability in their fairway driver collection. With a blunt nose reminiscent of the FD1 or FD3, its smoother margins on the edges and rim provide a comfortable grip for a powerful throw when you need it most. Whether you're executing a precise hyzer or anhyzer shot, the Vanguard delivers with moderate fade and dependable control, making it an essential disc in your arsenal.

name: Anax
description: The Anax (pronounced "Onyx") is Paul McBeth's new signature fairway/power driver. This 10-speed driver will fight the wind and stay straight down the fairway before a nice fade at the end. Slower arm speeds will find this disc to be very reliable for shorter fairway drives. Big arms can expect big distance out of the Anax.

name: Archer
description: The Discraft Archer is an understable midrange disc released during the 2016 Ace race. This is an excellent touch disc that with a very straight flight path with a hyzer flip. The Archer is a great all purpose disc for beginners that glides very well. It has a shallower hand feel with a microbead that smaller hands will love - perfect for women and younger players.

name: Ares
description: The Discraft Ares is a high-speed distance driver developed from the early Zeus 2.0 test flights. It was designed for players who wanted more torque resistance and stability than the original Zeus while maintaining the same comfortable hand feel and shot shaping ability. Those early test runs quickly gained attention among both professionals and everyday players for their dependable stability and strong finishing fade. The Ares keeps the familiar high-speed profile of the Zeus but introduces a more overstable, torque-resistant flight. It handles aggressive power, headwinds, and high-speed releases while continuing to push forward before finishing with a strong, reliable fade. Players can trust it on flex lines, powerful flat releases, and maximum distance drives where control and finish are critical. We'll have a full selection available on release day, April 3rd, so you can choose your exact weight and color. If you want to secure one sooner, the Ares is also available now for pre-order at Discount Disc Golf.

name: Athena
description: The Athena is the sixth disc in the Paul McBeth line from Discraft. It is an all-purpose fairway driver with a light end-fade. The Athena can be used when distance is not as important as landing with excellent control.

name: Avenger
description: The Discraft Avenger is an overstable distance driver good for intermediate and advanced players. It is one of the primary drivers that helped Nate Doss win the 2005 Pro World Championships. Discraft gives the Avenger a stability rating of 1.8. The Avenger is semi-out-of-production, and is only available with special releases, like Ledgestone Special Edition. If you're looking to try an Avenger, but can't seem to find one that suits your fancy, try the Anax. It's a very similar disc.

name: Avenger SS
description: The Avenger SS has become more popular than the mold that inspired it. The Discraft Avenger SS is the perfect disc for intermediate players who want more distance with their backhand throws. This understable disc is fast, but doesn't require much speed for max distance. Watch this disc turn, glide, and go farther than any other driver in your bag. Discraft gives the Avenger SS a stability rating of .5.

name: Banger GT
description: The Discraft Banger GT (Groove Top) is a unique Discraft putter that has a Groove around the top and a large bead that helps provide an enhanced grip. The Banger GT is very popular for hyzer putts and offers a consistent release. It is a slightly overstable putter with a very straight flight path. The Groove Top creates an interesting experience that makes the disc feel shallow, but still have that slow, chain grabbing flight of a deep dish putter. The unique Banger GT is one of the highest rated Discraft Discs .

name: Buzzz
description: The Buzzz (3 z's) is one of the most popular midrange discs in the world. This consistent, reliable, and stable disc will perform exactly how you want it to. With powerful throws the Buzzz will hold any line you put on it. With light, level throws, it will fade slightly. This super popular disc can be purchased in all of Discraft's plastics. In base line plastics it has a stability rating of 0. In premium plastic blends this disc is slightly overstable with a Discraft rating of .5.

name: Buzzz OS
description: The most popular mid-range in the world now has a more overstable brother — the Buzzz OS. This new disc has the same great feel that disc golfers have grown to love, with additional stability. The Buzzz OS is that perfect beadless flat-topped, overstable-but-workable midrange for forehands you've been looking for. With the lineup of Buzzz OS, Buzzz, and Buzzz SS, all your midrange needs are met.

name: Buzzz SS
description: The Buzzz SS is a less stable verzion of the ultra-popular Discraft Buzzz. This midrange has a similar feel to the Buzzz, with a rounder, smoother feeling rim. Players that want that Buzzz flight but can't quite get it to fly straight, or players that love the rounded, convex rim shape will love the Buzzz SS. Don't be fooled, this is not an understable midrange, like the Meteor or Comet. This is a straight flyer meant to be workable for low to even moderately high powered shots.

name: Captain's Raptor
description: The Captain's Raptor is a modified Raptor. It has a similar feel to the Raptor, with a flat top and a similar rim. This disc is designed to be more overstable than the Raptor, providing a beefier version that can slice through the air and provide an even more reliable flight. This is a disc that will be reliable in even more adverse weather conditions. Discraft plans to make this disc once a year. Each year it will feature a new stamp.

name: Captain's Thrasher
description: The Captain's Thrasher is a special annual release designed in collaboration with Team Discraft Co-Captain Missy Gannon. This takes the beloved Thrasher and modifies it for enhanced overstability, delivering reliable performance even in windy conditions. With its distinctive flight characteristics, the Captain's Thrasher offers seasoned players a powerful tool for achieving greater distance with precise control.

name: Challenger
description: The Challenger is a tall, overstable putter with a deep rim and a tiny bead. It has a Discraft stability rating of 1, and as an overstable putter is able to handle long approach shots without turning over. This putter is popular for advanced disc golfers. The Challenger is available in inexpensive Pro-D plastic, soft X plastic, and the high quality premium FLX plastic.

name: Challenger OS
description: The Challenger OS is a more overstable, flat-topped version of the popular Challenger. This putter works best for driving, throwing into headwinds, weaving forehand approaches, and tossing hyzer putts. It is overstable enough to meet the majority of a player's needs without feeling like a meat hook.

name: Challenger SS
description: The Challenger SS is a putter originally debuted as a "Test Flight" disc during the 2017 Ledgestone Open. It is a more understable version of the popular Challenger putter, with a flat-top, rounded edge and comfortable grip.

name: Cicada
description: The Discraft Cicada is designed to be a hyzer-flip machine. Beginners and intermediate players will love that with a smooth, controlled release, this disc will fly dead straight. With a smash, this disc will carve up long arcing turnovers and execute perfect rollers. As a proud addition to Discraft's lineup, the Cicada thrives, elevating the game of disc golf and complementing their legacy of excellence.

name: Cigarra
description: The Discraft Cigarra is an overstable evolution of the Cicada , designed in collaboration with Anthony Barela for players seeking more power, control, and distance. As Barela's first signature disc with Discraft, the Cigarra delivers enhanced stability, allowing throwers to confidently attack lines with increased torque resistance . Built for those who push their limits, this disc offers the perfect balance of reliability and speed.

name: Comet
description: The Discraft Comet is a very popular straight-flying, slightly understable midrange disc. This is a versatile disc that can handle all kinds of shots and conditions. Discraft gives the Comet a stability rating of 0. The Comet is available in the highly durable, premium ESP and Z plastics.

name: Crank
description: The Discraft Crank is a fast, stable driver suitable for big arm disc golfers looking for the right mix of stability and speed. It is described as a cross between the Nuke and the Nuke SS. Discraft has given the Crank a stability rating of 1.3, just enough stability in this disc to really let you "crank" it. Intermediate disc golfers who are looking for a longer drive will also enjoy this disc.

name: Crank SS
description: The Crank SS is designed to give disc golfers who don't have a lot of power more distance. More advanced disc golfers will find the Crank SS flips up easily on hyzer releases. Discraft gives the Crank SS a stability rating of .7

name: Crush
description: This disc is made for power throwers who really want to "Crush it" off the tee. The Discraft Crush is a super overstable disc for advanced players and forehand throws. The Crush is available in three different Discraft plastics, the inexpensive Pro-D, ultra-durable Z-Line, and premium ESP plastics.

name: Drive
description: The Discraft Drive is a distance driver in the Paige Pierce lineup. It has the perfect balance of precision and glide, slotting it right inbetween the Surge SS and Zeus. As an 11-speed distance driver, the Drive has the perfect combination of control and distance. It will an amazing distance driver for many players!

name: Drone
description: The Drone is not like it's stable cousin the Buzzz, but is actually one of the most overstable midrange discs on the market. Discraft gives the drone a stability rating of 2.5, making it a good midrange choice for advanced players and strategic hyzer throws. This is a disc that isn't going to turn over in a headwind.

name: Fierce
description: The Fierce is Paige Pierce's signature putter model. It's a beadless, understable putter with a grippy plastic feel. This putter is perfect for draining putts from any distance and works great for straight and anhyzer throws. Make the Discraft Fierce your new go-to putter!

name: Flick
description: If you're looking for a disc that won't turn over on your power forehand flicks, this disc is for you. The Flick can be thrown with big anhyzer angles, and it will always fade back. This disc is incredibly thin and has a completely flat top. This is an excellent disc for ultra-powerful forehand/sidearm shots and strategic fades. Discraft classifies the Flick in their "extra long range drivers" category but most find that it's overstable nature won't allow this disc to go "extra long". If you're looking for one of the most overstable and best forehand drivers , try the Discraft Flick.

name: Focus
description: The Focus is the perfect disc for disc golfers who really like to concentrate before throwing their disc in the chains. With a Discraft stability rating of .5, this slightly overstable putter provides a gentle fade and pinpoint accuracy for approach shots.

name: Force
description: Force your drives into maximum fade with this very overstable distance driver. The Discraft Force is one of the more overstable drivers, with a Discraft stability rating of 2.0. This disc has a wide rim (2.3cm) and travels at very high speeds. The force was built for power throwers looking for more distance, and is Discraft's fastest flying overstable driver. The Force is available in the inexpensive Pro-D plastic as well as high quality Elite Z, ESP, and Titanium plastics. If you throw with force, this is a disc you're going to want in your bag.

name: Fossil
description: The Discraft Ricky Wysocki Fossil is a prototype putt and approach disc designed for slow, overstable control. Built with a ringer-style top, subtle micro-bead, and reliable torque resistance, the Fossil offers a confident grip and consistent performance on both forehand and backhand throws. Its deliberate, steady pace makes it ideal for precise placement shots and short approaches that demand accuracy. Developed as Discraft's answer to the popular Innova Pig that Ricky made famous, the Fossil delivers the same trusted overstability in a fresh, tour-ready design.

name: Glide
description: Are you looking for a golf disc that will actually turn to the right on backhand throws? There are few slicing understable discs better than the Discraft Glide. While this is considered a fairway driver, new players will see maximum distance when throwing the Glide. The Glide is available in the the highly durable "Z" plastic. Discraft gives the Glide a stability rating of -.5. Players who really want to see an understable turn should purchase it in lower weights, between 164-169 grams.
